topic.ja: はじめて　あう　ひとに　あいさつ
topic.en: Greeting someone for the first time
topic.fr: Saluer quelqu'un pour la première fois
topic.es: Saludar a alguien por primera vez

ja.intro: はじめて　あう　ひとに　あいさつを　します。なんといいますか。
ja.question: なんといいますか。

tr.intro: İlk kez buluştuğunuz birine selam veriyorsunuz. Ne söylersiniz?

options.text: Bu ilk kez / İlk kez görüşüyoruz.

tr.question: Ne söylersiniz?

en.intro: You are greeting someone you are meeting for the first time. What do you say?

options.text: It's the first time / This is my first time.
Nice to meet you / How do you do.

en.question: What do you say?

de.intro: Sie begrüßen jemanden, den Sie zum ersten Mal treffen. Was sagen Sie?
de.question: Was sagen Sie?

fr.intro: Vous saluez quelqu'un que vous rencontrez pour la première fois. Que dites-vous?
fr.question: Que dites-vous?

es.intro: Saludas a alguien que conoces por primera vez. ¿Qué dices?
es.question: ¿Qué dices?

ko.intro: 처음 만나는 사람에게 인사를 합니다. 뭐라고 말합니까?
ko.question: 뭐라고 말합니까?

meanings.tr: memnun oldum / tanıştığımıza sevindim (ilk tanışmada söylenen standart selam)
meanings.en: nice to meet you / how do you do (standard greeting at a first meeting)
meanings.de: freut mich, Sie kennenzulernen (Standardgruß beim ersten Treffen)
meanings.fr: enchanté(e) (salutation standard lors d'une première rencontre)
meanings.es: mucho gusto / encantado (saludo estándar en un primer encuentro)
meanings.ko: 처음 뵙겠습니다 (처음 만날 때의 표준 인사말)

meanings.tr: ilk kez / ilk defa (zaman ya da sıra bildiren sıfat — selam ifadesi değil)
meanings.en: for the first time (adverb of time/order — not a greeting expression)
meanings.fr: pour la première fois (adverbe de temps — pas une expression de salutation)
meanings.es: por primera vez (adverbio de tiempo — no es una expresión de saludo)

grammar.point: はじめまして (初対面の挨拶)

meanings.tr: İlk tanışmada kullanılan ve sonrasında genellikle 'よろしくおねがいします' ile takip edilen standart kalıp.
meanings.en: Standard first-meeting greeting, usually followed by よろしくおねがいします.
meanings.de: Standardmäßiger Erstbegegnungsgruß, meist gefolgt von よろしくおねがいします.
meanings.fr: Salutation standard de première rencontre, généralement suivie de よろしくおねがいします.
meanings.es: Saludo estándar de primer encuentro, generalmente seguido de よろしくおねがいします.
meanings.ko: 처음 만날 때 쓰는 표준 인사말로 보통 뒤에 よろしくおねがいします가 따라옵니다.

logic.tr: Ok karakteri ilk kez birini tanıyan kişidir. '2. はじめまして' (Memnun oldum.) doğrudur — ilk tanışmada söylenen standart ifadedir. Seçenek 1 'はじめてです' 'ilk kez' anlamında bir zaman ifadesidir; selam kalıbı değildir (Bağlam Karışıklığı). Seçenek 3 'はじめますね' 'başlıyoruz' anlamındadır; tanışma selamıyla ilgisi yoktur (Bağlam Karışıklığı).
logic.en: The arrow character is meeting someone for the first time. '2. はじめまして' is correct — standard first-meeting greeting. Option 1 'はじめてです' means 'it's the first time' — not a greeting formula (Context Confusion). Option 3 'はじめますね' means 'let's begin' — unrelated to introductions (Context Confusion).
logic.de: '2. はじめまして' ist korrekt. Option 1 'はじめてです' bedeutet 'es ist das erste Mal' — keine Begrüßungsformel (Kontextverwechslung). Option 3 'はじめますね' bedeutet 'fangen wir an' (Kontextverwechslung).
logic.fr: '2. はじめまして' est correct. L'option 1 'はじめてです' signifie 'c'est la première fois' — pas une formule de salutation (confusion de contexte). L'option 3 'はじめますね' signifie 'on commence' (confusion de contexte).
logic.es: '2. はじめまして' es correcto. La opción 1 'はじめてです' significa 'es la primera vez' — no es una fórmula de saludo (confusión de contexto). La opción 3 'はじめますね' significa 'empezamos' (confusión de contexto).
logic.ko: '2. はじめまして'가 정답입니다. 선택지 1 'はじめてです'는 '처음이에요'라는 시간 표현으로 인사 공식이 아닙니다(상황 혼동). 선택지 3 'はじめますね'는 '시작합니다'라는 뜻으로 소개와 관련이 없습니다(상황 혼동).

image_prompt: Simple manga-style line drawing, black and white. Two business people with simple round heads facing each other. The person on the LEFT bows slightly with a polite smile. The person on the RIGHT also bows slightly, smiling back. Both wear formal clothes. The scene conveys a first meeting or introduction. Cartoon style with simple round faces, clean outlines, white background, no shading, no color, no text, no arrows. Same style as JLPT N5 textbook manga illustrations.
